DECLARATION OF ETHAN JACOBS IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT (TRANSACTION ID # 100103746) FILED BY DEFENDANT JAMES JR., DONALD W

LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, JUL-14-2020 HEARING HELD RE: DEFENDANT DONALD JAMES JR.'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE DEFAULT. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT DONALD W. JAMES, JR.'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST HIM ON FEBRUARY 20, 2020 IS GRANTED ON THE CONDITIONS THAT WITHIN 5 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF THE COURT'S ORDER, HE PAY PLAINTIFF $1,000 AND FILE AN ANSWER. IF EITHER CONDITION IS NOT COMPLIED WITH, THE DEFAULT WILL REMAIN IN EFFECT, AND PLAINTIFF MAY PROCEED WITH THE SCHEDULED JULY 21 PROVE-UP HEARING. ORDER SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR COMPLETE RULING.) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: SEAN KANE; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR#5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM (302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DONALD JAMES JR.'S MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-21-590620 WITH CASE CGC-20-582135. ARGUMENT IS HELD. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DONALD'S JAMES MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE CASES CGC-21-590620 AND CGC-20-582135 IS GRANTED FOR ALL PURPOSES. (CCP 1048(A).) THE CASES ALLEGEDLY INVOLVE THE PURCHASE, RENOVATION AND SALE OF THE SAME HOUSE. CGC-21-590620 SHALL BE THE LEAD CASE. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

DEFENDANT DONALD JAMES JR.'s MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-21-590620 WITH CASE CGC-20-582135.

The matter is called. ARGUMENT is held. At the conclusion of argument, the tentative ruling is adopted as follows:

Defendant Donald's James motion to consolidate cases CGC-21-590620 and CGC-20-582135 is granted for all purposes. (CCP 1048(a).) The cases allegedly involve the purchase, renovation and sale of the same house. CGC-21-590620 shall be the lead case.

Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of David C. Macpherson Regarding Meet And Confer
Off Calendar Mar-11-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF TRACY MCLAUGHLIN's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings.

No appearance in this matter. The Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's common law motion for judgment on the pleadings is granted with 20 days leave to amend. Defendant has leave to plead ultimate facts to support his defenses. (See FPI Development, Inc vs. A1 Nakashima (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 367, 384 [an affirmative defense must be pleaded with the same care as a cause of action].) Defendant is admonished to plead cognizable defenses. For example, the equitable defense of laches does not apply to a legal claim for breach of contract (see id.; Abbott v. City of Los Angeles (1958) 50 Cal.2d 438, 461-462) and some of the defenses pleaded ("frivolous claims") are not cognizable.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported (302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate And Set Aside Default
Off Calendar Jul-14-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Atty. DAVID C. MACPHERSON, Esq. of the Law Offices of David C. Macpherson (Tel: 415-924- 1235) for plaintiff Tracy Mclaughlin;
Atty. ETHAN JACOBS, Esq. of Holland Law LLP (Tel: 415-200-4984) for defendant Donald W. James, Jr.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT DONALD JAMES JR.'S Motion to Vacate and Set Aside Default.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling: Defendant Donald W. James, Jr.'s motion to vacate and set aside the default entered against him on February 20, 2020 is granted on the conditions that within 5 days of notice of entry of the court's order, he pay plaintiff $1,000 and file an answer. If either condition is not complied with, the default will remain in effect, and plaintiff may proceed with the scheduled July 21 prove-up hearing.

The process server swears that on January 15, 2020, he personally served Mr. James. (Klein Decl. 3.) Defendant claims that the process server left the papers with his eight-year-old daughter. (James Decl. 6.) The record shows that on January 21, 2020, an attorney acting on defendant's behalf sought an open extension of time to file an answer to the complaint on defendant's behalf, and did not assert that his client had not been properly served or that he was contesting the adequacy of service. (MacPherson Decl. 3.) Likewise, on February 14, 2020, Mr. James contacted plaintiff's counsel himself to let him know that he was "interviewing attorneys to represent [him]," and again sought an extension of time, which plaintiff's counsel declined to grant. (Id. 5 & Ex. A.) Again, Mr. James made no claim in that communication that he had not been properly served. Plaintiff waited an additional six days before taking defendant's default, and defendant then waited more than three months, until June 5, to file the instant motion. In light of these facts, the court is skeptical of Mr. James' account, and also doubts that Mr. James has acted with the required reasonable diligence. (See, e.g., Stafford v. Mach (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1174, 1184 [delays of three months or more routinely result in denial of relief where there is no satisfactory explanation for the delay].) In light of California's strong policy favoring the resolution of cases on their merits and the intervening COVID-19 public health emergency, however, it will nevertheless grant the motion on the stated conditions.

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR#5041, email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com (302/EPS)
